overview: A microservices architecture with a React frontend (leave-web-app), a Go backend API (leave-service) managing employee leave requests, approvals, and balances, and a new notification service (notification-service) that sends email/SMS notifications when leave requests are submitted, approved, or rejected. The frontend communicates with the backend via REST API, the backend stores leave requests and user data in memory, and the backend publishes events to the notification service for asynchronous notification delivery.
requirements: Employees must be able to submit leave requests specifying start date, end date, and leave type
Managers must be able to view pending leave requests from their direct reports only
Managers must be able to approve or reject leave requests
Employees must be able to view the status of their submitted leave requests (pending, approved, rejected)
All users must be able to view their current leave balance by leave type
The system must enforce manager-employee reporting relationships
The system must support multiple leave types (e.g., annual, sick, personal)
The system must provide health check endpoints for all services
The backend API must be documented with OpenAPI specification
The frontend must be a single-page application accessible via web browser
The system must send notifications to employees when their leave request is submitted
The system must send notifications to managers when a new leave request is pending their approval
The system must send notifications to employees when their leave request is approved or rejected
Notifications must support multiple channels (email, SMS)

components.name: leave-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Management API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Backend API for managing employee leave requests and approvals
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /api/v1/users/me:
    get:
      summary: Get current user profile
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Current user details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/User'
  /api/v1/leave-requests:
    get:
      summary: List leave requests
      description: Employees see their own requests, managers see their team's requests
      parameters:
        - name: status
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of leave requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
    post:
      summary: Create a new leave request
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - startDate
                - endDate
                - leaveType
              properties:
                startDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                endDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                leaveType:
                  type: string
                  enum: [annual, sick, personal]
                reason:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Leave request created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '400':
          description: Invalid request data
  /api/v1/leave-requests/{id}:
    get:
      summary: Get a specific leave request
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '404':
          description: Leave request not found
  /api/v1/leave-requests/{id}/approve:
    post:
      summary: Approve a leave request (managers only)
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request approved
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '403':
          description: Not authorized to approve
        '404':
          description: Leave request not found
  /api/v1/leave-requests/{id}/reject:
    post:
      summary: Reject a leave request (managers only)
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                reason:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request rejected
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '403':
          description: Not authorized to reject
        '404':
          description: Leave request not found
  /api/v1/leave-balance:
    get:
      summary: Get current user's leave balance by type
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave balance by type
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  annual:
                    type: number
                    format: float
                  sick:
                    type: number
                    format: float
                  personal:
                    type: number
                    format: float
components:
  schemas:
    User:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        email:
          type: string
        name:
          type: string
        role:
          type: string
          enum: [employee, manager]
        managerId:
          type: string
    LeaveRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        employeeId:
          type: string
        employeeName:
          type: string
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        leaveType:
          type: string
          enum: [annual, sick, personal]
        reason:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        reviewedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        reviewedBy:
          type: string
        rejectionReason:
          type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go backend service using net/http that listens on port 9090. Use an in-memory data store with predefined seed data for users, leave balances, and leave requests. Define User struct with id, email, name, role (employee or manager), and managerId. Define LeaveRequest struct matching the OpenAPI schema. Seed at least 3 employees and 1 manager with reporting relationships. Initialize leave balances (annual: 20 days, sick: 10 days, personal: 5 days per employee). Implement GET /health returning {"status":"ok"}. Implement GET /api/v1/users/me to return current user based on a mock user ID from header X-User-Id (default to employee1 if missing). Implement GET /api/v1/leave-requests: if user is employee, return only their requests; if manager, return requests from direct reports. Support optional query param status to filter. Implement POST /api/v1/leave-requests to create a new request with status pending, validate dates and leave type, generate unique ID, store it. Implement GET /api/v1/leave-requests/{id} to retrieve a single request if user has access. Implement POST /api/v1/leave-requests/{id}/approve: check if current user is a manager and the request belongs to their direct report, update status to approved, set reviewedAt and reviewedBy. Implement POST /api/v1/leave-requests/{id}/reject: same authorization as approve, update status to rejected, store optional rejection reason. Implement GET /api/v1/leave-balance to return current user's balance by leave type. Use JSON for all request/response bodies. Return appropriate HTTP status codes (200, 201, 400, 403, 404). Log all requests. Use standard library only, no external dependencies except for routing if needed (can use net/http ServeMux). Ensure thread-safe access to in-memory data with sync.RWMutex. Provide a Dockerfile with multi-stage build using golang:1.21 as builder and alpine as runtime. Expose port 9090.

components.name: leave-web-app
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Management Web Application
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Frontend single-page application for leave management
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:3000
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Web app is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a React single-page application using TypeScript, React 18, and Vite as the build tool. Create a responsive UI with the following pages: Dashboard (shows user info and leave balance), My Requests (employee view of their leave requests with status), Team Requests (manager view of pending requests from direct reports), and New Request (form to submit a leave request). Use React Router for navigation. Fetch data from leave-service at http://leave-service:9090/api/v1 (configure via environment variable VITE_API_BASE_URL). Send X-User-Id header with every API request (mock user selection dropdown in dev mode or read from a cookie). Use axios or fetch for API calls. Display leave balance as cards showing available days per leave type (annual, sick, personal). For My Requests page, show a table with columns: dates, type, status, reason, and action buttons (view details). For Team Requests page (visible only to managers), show pending requests with employee name and approve/reject buttons. New Request page should have a form with date pickers for start/end date, dropdown for leave type, textarea for reason, and submit button. Validate that end date is after start date. Show success/error toasts after actions. Use a simple CSS framework like Tailwind CSS or Material-UI for styling. Implement GET /health endpoint in the Express or static server serving the React app, returning {"status":"ok"}. Provide a Dockerfile with multi-stage build: build stage uses node:18 to run npm install and npm run build, runtime stage uses nginx:alpine to serve the built static files. Copy build output to /usr/share/nginx/html. Expose port 3000. Configure nginx to route API requests to leave-service if needed, or handle CORS in the frontend. Include environment variable substitution for API base URL at runtime.

components.name: notification-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Notification Service API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Service for sending notifications via email and SMS
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /api/v1/notifications:
    post:
      summary: Send a notification
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - recipient
                - subject
                - message
                - channels
              properties:
                recipient:
                  type: object
                  required:
                    - email
                  properties:
                    email:
                      type: string
                    phone:
                      type: string
                subject:
                  type: string
                message:
                  type: string
                channels:
                  type: array
                  items:
                    type: string
                    enum: [email, sms]
                metadata:
                  type: object
                  additionalProperties: true
      responses:
        '202':
          description: Notification accepted for delivery
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  id:
                    type: string
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: queued
        '400':
          description: Invalid request data
components:
  schemas:
    Notification:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        recipient:
          type: object
          properties:
            email:
              type: string
            phone:
              type: string
        subject:
          type: string
        message:
          type: string
        channels:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [queued, sent, failed]
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        sentAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go backend service using net/http that listens on port 9090. Implement GET /health returning {"status":"ok"}. Implement POST /api/v1/notifications that accepts a notification request with recipient (email and optional phone), subject, message, channels array (email and/or sms), and optional metadata. Validate the request payload. Generate a unique notification ID and return 202 Accepted with {"id":"<uuid>","status":"queued"}. Use an in-memory queue or goroutine to asynchronously process notifications. For email channel, log the email details (recipient email, subject, message) to stdout with prefix [EMAIL]. For sms channel, log the SMS details (recipient phone, message) to stdout with prefix [SMS]. Do not integrate with real email/SMS providers; simulation via logging is sufficient. Store notification records in-memory with fields: id, recipient, subject, message, channels, status (queued, sent, failed), createdAt, sentAt. After logging, update status to sent and set sentAt timestamp. Handle errors gracefully and set status to failed if logging fails. Use JSON for request/response bodies. Return appropriate HTTP status codes (202, 400). Use standard library only, no external dependencies except for UUID generation (can use google/uuid or crypto/rand). Ensure thread-safe access to in-memory data with sync.RWMutex. Provide a Dockerfile with multi-stage build using golang:1.21 as builder and alpine as runtime. Expose port 9090. The service is called by leave-service after leave request events (submission, approval, rejection) to send notifications to employees and managers.
